title: Mildly Pungent Chinese Monkfish Sweet And Sour
description: A lightly pungent and flavorful Chinese dish from the Zhejiang region, featuring monkfish in a sweet and sour sauce, made with beurre-monte for a rich and creamy texture, and prepared in a Buddhist-temple-friendly manner.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the monkfish pieces and cook until they are browned and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and cornstarch.
Pour the sauce into the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 2 minutes, until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the beurre-monte until it is fully incorporated and the sauce is smooth.
Add the chopped scallions and cook for 1 minute, until they are slightly wilted.
Serve the monkfish and sauce over steamed rice or noodles.
